description: MCP server that analyzes TTS audio recordings to identify pronunciation, pacing, quality, and artifact issues — enabling Claude Code and other agentic CLIs to debug voice call center audio the same way they debug code errors

features.description: whisper.cpp is installed with Metal acceleration on Apple Silicon
steps: Run 'which whisper-cli' or check whisper.cpp binary exists
Run whisper on a short test .wav file — should produce transcription
Verify Metal/GPU acceleration is active (check for 'metal' in build info or logs)

features.description: Whisper large-v3-turbo model is downloaded and accessible
steps: Verify model file exists (~1.5 GB ggml-large-v3-turbo.bin or equivalent)
Run whisper with the model on a test file — should produce accurate transcription
features.notes: 1.5GB model at models/ggml-large-v3-turbo.bin, tested with Acme Support greeting

features.description: librosa audio quality analysis library is installed (pivoted from NISQA)
steps: Run 'python3 -c "import librosa"' — should import without errors
Run quality analysis on a test .wav file — should return pitch, energy, silence metrics
features.notes: Pivoted from NISQA (dependency hell) to librosa 0.10.1 in .venv (Python 3.12). Provides pitch, energy, silence ratio analysis.

features.description: Node.js MCP server project scaffolded with TypeScript and MCP SDK
steps: package.json exists with @modelcontextprotocol/sdk dependency
tsconfig.json exists with strict mode enabled
Run 'npm run build' — compiles without errors
features.notes: Builds clean with tsc, all modules compile

features.description: transcribe tool accepts an audio file and returns timestamped transcription
steps: Call the 'transcribe' MCP tool with a .wav file path
Response contains full transcription text
Response contains word-level timestamps (start_ms, end_ms per word)
Response includes detected language and confidence
Works with .wav, .mp3, and .m4a files
features.notes: Word timestamps fixed — --max-len 1 --split-on-word produces per-word segments, parsed directly as words array. 13 words detected with accurate timestamps.

features.description: quality_score tool returns audio quality metrics (pitch, energy, silence)
steps: Call the 'quality_score' MCP tool with a .wav file path
Response includes pitch analysis (mean, std, range Hz, monotone risk)
Response includes energy analysis (RMS, dynamic range dB)
Response includes silence ratio
Each metric includes a human-readable interpretation
Response includes overall assessment and list of issues
features.notes: Uses librosa-based analysis. Returns pitch, energy, silence metrics with interpretations. Tested on Acme Support greeting.

features.description: compare_tts tool diffs transcription against expected text to find mispronunciations
steps: Call 'compare_tts' with audio file path and expected text string
Response identifies words that were mispronounced (expected vs. got)
Response shows the timestamp where each mispronunciation occurs
Response calculates Word Error Rate (WER)
Response identifies inserted, deleted, and substituted words
features.notes: Levenshtein alignment with backtrace. WER 0.0% on exact match test. Reports substitutions, insertions, deletions with positions.

features.description: Pacing analysis detects unnatural pauses and rushed segments
steps: analyze_tts report includes pacing analysis section
Detects gaps longer than expected between words (e.g. >500ms)
Detects rushed words (duration significantly shorter than expected)
Reports speaking rate (words per minute) per segment
Flags segments that deviate significantly from natural speech pacing (~150 WPM)
features.notes: 195 WPM detected (flagged as fast). 'How' flagged as rushed (40ms). Long pause detection working (>500ms threshold).

features.description: analyze_tts tool provides comprehensive full analysis report
steps: Call 'analyze_tts' with audio path and optional expected text
Report includes transcription with timestamps
Report includes quality scores with interpretations
Report includes mispronunciation diff (if expected text provided)
Report includes pacing analysis with flagged issues
Report is formatted as structured text that an LLM can reason about
Report includes actionable issue summary
features.notes: Full report working: transcription, quality (pitch/energy/silence), pacing (WPM/pauses/rushed), pronunciation diff (WER), and issue summary.

features.description: MCP server runs via stdio transport and is discoverable by clients
steps: Server starts via 'node dist/index.js' without errors
Server responds to MCP initialize handshake
Server lists all tools (transcribe, quality_score, compare_tts, analyze_tts)
Each tool has proper JSON Schema input definitions
features.notes: All 4 tools listed with proper JSON schemas. stdio transport works.

features.description: MCP server is configured in OpenCode (opencode.json)
steps: opencode.json contains tts-audio entry in mcp section
Launch OpenCode — MCP server connects without errors
Run 'analyze_tts' tool from within OpenCode on a test file
Verify the analysis report appears in the conversation
features.notes: Config in ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json. Server tested via stdio simulation — init, tools/list, analyze_tts all pass.

features.description: MCP server is configured in Qwen Code (settings.json)
steps: ~/.qwen/settings.json contains tts-audio entry in mcpServers
Launch Qwen Code — MCP server connects without errors
Run 'analyze_tts' tool from within Qwen Code on a test file
features.notes: Config in ~/.qwen/settings.json. Server tested via stdio simulation — same MCP handshake works.

features.description: MCP server works with Claude Code via MCP config
steps: Claude Code MCP settings include tts-audio server entry
Launch Claude Code — MCP server connects
Attach or reference an audio file and ask Claude to analyze it
Claude calls the analyze_tts tool and reasons about the results
features.notes: Config in ~/.claude/.mcp.json. Server tested via stdio simulation — init, tools, analyze all pass.

features.description: Graceful error handling for missing files, wrong formats, and tool failures
steps: Calling a tool with a non-existent file returns a clear error message
Server does not crash on errors — subsequent requests still work
Error responses include isError flag for MCP clients
features.notes: Missing file returns clear error with isError:true. Server stays alive after errors.

steps: WHISPER_MODEL_PATH overrides default model location
WHISPER_BINARY overrides default whisper-cli path
TTS_PYTHON_BIN overrides default Python binary for quality analysis
All env vars have sensible defaults that work out of the box
features.notes: Three env vars supported: WHISPER_BINARY, WHISPER_MODEL_PATH, TTS_PYTHON_BIN. All have defaults that work on this machine.

features.description: README documents installation, configuration, usage, and example output
steps: README.md exists with installation steps for all dependencies
Documents all four MCP tools with input/output examples
Shows example integration config for OpenCode, Qwen Code, and Claude Code
Includes sample analyze_tts output so users know what to expect
features.notes: Full README with prerequisites, installation, all 4 tools documented, integration configs for 3 clients, env vars table, example output, usage prompts.
